rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required. Plaintiff has demonstrated that evidence exists that vitopac and Victor Gaskets were routinely used on Navy ships including the Trathan, that the product came in sheets and when applied, asbestos was released. The Clowser declaration, Ex. K, is further evidence on this point. What is uncertain is whether plaintiff is able to obtain evidence from Mr. Scheoder that places decedent in or around defendant's gaskets when this work was being done. If submitted by declaration, the declarant must "declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California" that the information is true and correct. The Court has not received any further declaration from Mr. Jones. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CARNELL GAINES et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARNELL GAINES et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. Plaintiff must demonstrate why witnesses Riley and Jimenez were not identified in the special interrogatories. If plaintiff demonstrates that the omission was not willful (see Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corp. (2004) 124 Cal. App. 4th 1315, 1324-25), the Court finds the substance of the declarations admissible and when coupled with the Ay declaration, plaintiff has presented evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD WILSON et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence based on factually devoid discovery. Plaintiff lacks evidence that the off-loaded product was actually shipped to defendant and defendant cannot be liable for this product until defendant receives it. Until that time, defendant is not a manufacturer, distributor or supplier. Plaintiff's counsel is advised that henceforth any brief in excess of the page limit, the excess pages will not be considered. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BERNARD ROGOVER VS. A. P. GREEN INDUSTRIES, INC. (A.P. GRREN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court may consider plaintiff's deposition testimony under Villa v. McFerrin (1995) 35 Cal. App. 4th 733. Defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses are factually devoid. The answers merely list job sites with a general description of the work done and products present. Plant is described as being a contractor on certain jobs co-workers are identified without contact information or any indication what they know. Plaintiff's deposition testimony lacks the requisite specifics for a trier of fact to conclude that defendant's employees did anything that exposed plaintiff to asbestos. The Court fails to see how defendants P M K can do so. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CAROLAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant shifted the burden. Plaintiff creates a triable issue of fact based on Speck declaration. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CAROLAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to August 29, 2008 per amended notice. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CAROLAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 31, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The motion is based on plaintiff's alleged inability to link decedent to defendant's product. Defendant shifted the burden and plaintiff created a triable issue of fact on the exposure issue by declarations of Speck and Schoezel. In moving for summary judgment, defendant did not place at issue whether decedent was exposed to original asbestos in defendant's product. =(302/PJM/PB)
